what_it_does: This file is a generated CSS token sheet that defines a global :root design system and two scoped namespaces (.tkdrawer and .gmdrawer) for the surface layer, exposing colors, spacing, typography, radii, motion, shadows, layout margins, and device dimensions as CSS custom properties.
observations: Header states the file is GENERATED from dna/tokens.json and dna/grammar.json and should not be edited by hand
Defines an identity palette plus semantic marks, scaffold inks, a warm-pole ramp, and chrome-only shell tones
Uses color-mix() to derive --gold-deep and --reject-deep from palette colors
Includes a large explicit spacing scale based on --dna-unit:8px and a 1.5 ratio
Defines a granular type scale with fractional sizes and underscore-style token names
Provides motion durations, soft/float shadows, an elevation ladder, and a layout margin clamp
Embeds a device registry with mobile/tablet/desktop viewport dimensions
Provides .tkdrawer and .gmdrawer namespaces that alias the global palette variables
standouts: The prominent 'GENERATED — do not edit by hand' provenance comment and rebuild command
The 'one source, every surface' claim tying surface chrome to DNA tokens
Use of color-mix() for derived accent colors
The elevation ladder with named lift/recess/hero shadow recipes
Embedding application device dimensions directly in CSS custom properties
conventions: CSS custom properties declared globally in :root
Comments annotate source provenance and semantic intent
Token names carry a 'dna-' prefix to indicate generated system origin
Color tokens separate identity palette from chrome-only shell tones
concerns: A long list of spacing/radius/type tokens may contain values that are never consumed in this file
Generated file discourages hand editing, so any local fix must be made in the source JSON instead
No fallback values for custom properties if tokens are missing
Type token names use underscores (e.g. --dna-type-6_5) which is legal but inconsistent with hyphen-only spacing/radius tokens
notes: A generated, read-only CSS token substrate that materializes the project's DNA palette, scale, motion, and device registry into custom properties for surface styling.
questions: Which components actually consume the full spacing and type scales defined here?
Why do some type tokens use underscores while spacing and radius tokens do not?
Is the device registry used by CSS container queries, JS layout logic, or both?
What is the intended difference between .tkdrawer and .gmdrawer consumers?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to derive surface chrome from DNA tokens; the file does exactly that by declaring the resulting CSS custom-property registry.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth from design tokens
Systematic modular scales for space, type, and radius
Clear separation between identity palette and chrome-only tones
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the surface layer a deterministic, rebuildable projection of a central token/grammar source, so visual consistency can be enforced through generation rather than manual CSS edits.
novelty: It embeds an explicit build provenance, an elevation-ladder shadow recipe, and a device registry inside a generated CSS token file, and it scopes palette aliases under separate tokens-drawer and grammar-drawer namespaces.
contribution: It contributes a complete, named CSS custom-property vocabulary for color, spacing, typography, motion, shadow, layout margin, and device dimensions, plus scoped palette aliases that let different surface contexts reference the same underlying tokens.
summary_for_embedding: A generated CSS file that exposes the project's DNA-derived design tokens as :root custom properties and scoped .tkdrawer/.gmdrawer aliases for surface-layer styling.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, rebuildable, token-driven CSS substrate that keeps the surface chrome synchronized with the central DNA token and grammar definitions.
